phases.goal: Understand your confidence patterns and start expanding your comfort zone

steps.title: Map your confidence landscape
steps.description: List 10 situations you find difficult: speaking in meetings, introducing yourself, asserting your needs, dating, public speaking, etc. Rate each 1–10 for anxiety level. This becomes your roadmap — you'll work from lowest to highest over 12 weeks.
steps.success_metric: Confidence anxiety map completed with 10+ situations rated

steps.title: Start a confidence journal
steps.description: Each evening, write down one moment where you acted despite feeling uncertain. No matter how small. Over time this builds a concrete evidence base that you are, in fact, capable — which is the foundation of real confidence.
steps.success_metric: Daily confidence journal maintained for 21 consecutive days

steps.title: Take one small uncomfortable action per day
steps.description: Start from the low end of your anxiety map. Say hello to a stranger. Ask a question in a meeting. Send the email you've been avoiding. Each small act of courage rewires your brain's threat response slightly. Consistency compounds.
steps.success_metric: One uncomfortable action taken every day for 3 weeks

phases.goal: Build the specific skills that create visible confidence

steps.title: Join Toastmasters or a public speaking group
steps.description: Public speaking is rated more feared than death. It's also the most transferable confidence skill. Toastmasters provides a structured environment to practise regularly with feedback. Attend for at least 6 weeks before judging the impact.
steps.success_metric: Joined Toastmasters or equivalent and attended for 6 consecutive weeks

steps.title: Improve body language deliberately
steps.description: Confidence communicates before you speak. Work on: standing tall, maintaining eye contact, speaking at a deliberate pace, and not filling silence with nervous sounds. Film yourself speaking for 2 minutes. The gap between how you feel and how you look is usually significant.
steps.success_metric: Self-filmed speaking exercise done twice and one specific body language habit changed

steps.title: Progressively tackle harder items on your anxiety map
steps.description: Each week, intentionally approach one item that sits above your current comfort level. The goal isn't to feel no anxiety — it's to act despite it. Each repeated exposure reduces the anxiety around that situation permanently.
steps.success_metric: At least 6 items from your anxiety map deliberately confronted

phases.goal: Cement your new self-image and maintain momentum

steps.title: Rewrite your self-narrative
steps.description: The story you tell about yourself shapes your behaviour. Replace 'I'm shy' with 'I'm becoming someone who speaks up'. Write out 5 new identity statements in present tense: 'I am someone who...' This isn't self-deception — it's directing your brain where to grow.
steps.success_metric: 5 new identity statements written and reviewed daily for 2 weeks

steps.title: Do the thing that scared you most
steps.description: Return to the top of your anxiety map. The item that felt impossible in week 1. By now, your comfort zone has expanded dramatically. Doing this item is not just a milestone — it proves to yourself that you are fundamentally different.
